subtitle: Cathedral of Rust and Code
quote: Wakey wakey, eggs and bakey. Reality subroutine glitching.

ciphers.encoding_hint: the infinity drawn by Frasier's soldering iron is composed of binary fragments — read clockwise from the right loop's top

ciphers.text: miniature red steamboat in the model city Frasier is wiring
ciphers.encoding_hint: Frasier is modeling/predicting D'Ambrosio's empire BEFORE meeting the Emperor — the red steamboat in his diorama is the same boat from the IV - EMPEROR card

ciphers.text: two warehouse demons visible in the periphery
ciphers.encoding_hint: demon count maps to portrait_the_demon.json — pair = the demonic infestation Frasier is debugging

ciphers.text: lower-right CRT monitor displaying live biometric/process readout
ciphers.encoding_hint: matches the empress.json::ems_biometrics_alert overlay structure — same diagnostic system, different chapter

ciphers.text: paint can on the desk reading 'COUNTY ROAD' (partial / illegible)
ciphers.encoding_hint: the partial label is a route reference — full text appears in vol5_ch3_empress's prose as Nicola walks past the abandoned warehouses

ciphers.text: Frasier's CRT now scrolls the same particle-sync waveform from the Fool's IV - DEMOSCENE_VIBE.EXE label
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until you've read the demoscene module label on the Fool card. The two scenes share the same render engine — the diner's classified-ad layout and Frasier's CRT are two windows on the same broadcast. Once you've seen it on one card, it's been there on the other all along.

ciphers.text: a tiny lion-tamer figure in the model city, infinity sigil floating above her
ciphers.encoding_hint: DORMANT until the Strength card's lemniscate halo is recognized. Frasier draws the sigil; Strength wears it; the model city he has been wiring shows her wearing it back. The Magician's diorama renders the future of his own inscription — I → VIII is the deck's longest reach across one symbol. The classical 'as above, so below' is here as 'as inscribed, so worn.'

hotspots.notes: DORMANT: appears only after the player has read the IV - DEMOSCENE_VIBE.EXE label on the Fool card. Frasier's CRT now displays the same particle-sync waveform the Fool's diner classified-ad pointed at — the two cards share the same render engine.
with the bindle armed, the partial 'COUNTY ROAD' label on the paint can completes against a hand-drawn map John has been carrying — the two halves of the route finally meet.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has recognized the lemniscate halo on the Strength card. The infinity Frasier traces is now visibly being WORN by a figure in the distant model city — a tiny lion-tamer on the diorama, sigil floating above. The Magician drew the glyph; Strength wears it; the Magician can now see her wearing it. The deck closes the inscription loop. I → VIII is the deck's longest reach across one symbol.

ENTRY HOTSPOT: clicking the painted model city dispatches demon_scribe.01 to fetch the full top-down view + Frasier's annotations on each element. 9 model elements (steamboat, warehouse 47, st. jude's miniature, ember & ash, daigle's pier, render farm site (empty), empress chamber, anya's block (unwired), infinity sigil overhead). Click any element to read Frasier's notes on what it predicts. The model is the deck's predictive architecture rendered as inventory.

DORMANT: appears only after the Emperor's Frasier-painting hotspot fires (the painting is found on Dante's wall). The upper-left of Frasier's workshop now visibly carries a small worn paper pinned to the rust girders — a graphite sketch labelled 'study 4 — waiter cosmic — D'Ambrosio diner — for the larger painting.' The graphite study is unmistakably John Frank's posture. Frasier's notes pinned beside the sketch read in his hand: 'I painted him cosmic because he is, and because the family is paid to forget that he is. I gave the painting to D. on the way out because the painting is the argument I refused to make in person. He kept the painting. He says he fired me. I say I quit. Both of us preserve the disagreement; the painting preserves the subject. Three witnesses, three readings, one canvas. The painting is the deck's only honest portrait of an employee of this household. The deck's working method is render the worker as cosmic and let the employer keep the indictment.' → wakes content on Fool (0): John doesn't know the painting exists.

DORMANT: appears only after the Empress Aria-log diorama surfaces the routing handoff. The lower-right CRT on Frasier's workbench now visibly shows a small text annotation in the lower-left corner: 'routing received: aria.runtime / source: chamber_dinner_3 / spike registered.' Frasier received the trace at 19:20 local; he did not know it was Nicola; he knew it was someone. The Magician chapter's Frasier&Aria pair-lattice entry is the diagnostic he can read but not name. I ↔ III handshake confirmed from both endpoints.

DORMANT: appears only after the player has recognized the multiplied gesture on the Judgement card. Frasier's solo 'as above, so below' posture now visibly shows a faint chorus of other arms behind him — the rest of the ensemble pre-rendered as ghost-figures in his rust girders. I ↔ XX mirror reads both directions: Judgement multiplied the gesture; Magician was already implying it. The chapter at I knew the ensemble at XX was coming.

easter_eggs: the infinity symbol Frasier traces is the SAME glyph the Priestess card hides in her shelf books — Magician (active) creates it, Priestess (passive) reads it
model city contains predictive placement of D'Ambrosio's empire BEFORE Frasier has met Dante — implies the magician sees forward
subtle green data-stream embedded in the rust girders mirrors the emerald-green data overlay from Empress (Aria's POV) — Frasier and Aria operate in the same medium
CRT in lower-right shows what looks like a heart rate trace — Frasier is monitoring something/someone remotely
